meaning: n.开塞钻,螺丝锥adj.拔钻形的,螺旋形的vt.硬拉出,探听vi.作螺旋状前进
examples: It's a corkscrew and bottle-opener all in one.
Smee whispered, fidgeting with Johnny Corkscrew.
"Shall I after him, Captain," asked pathetic Smee, "and tickle him with Johnny Corkscrew?"
Dr Benford reckons the Snake is actually something like a corkscrew shape seen side-on.
When technology was sold and run in silos, the corkscrew approach to staffing was the only way.
